article_title: Humanitarian Organizations Turn to AI and Corporate Partnerships as USAID Funding Collapse Reshapes Global Aid

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: edition.cnn.com

content_preview.title: Humanitarian Organizations Turn to AI and Corporate Partnerships as USAID Funding Collapse Reshapes Global Aid
content_preview.summary: As USAID funding collapses by more than half, aid organizations deploy AI tools and corporate partnerships to stretch diminished resources across a record 300 million people in need.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
The dismantling of the United States Agency for International Development has triggered the most severe contraction of global humanitarian funding in modern history, forcing relief organizations to accelerate technology adoption and seek private-sector partnerships at an unprecedented pace.

U.S. foreign aid spending [fell from $68 billion in 2024 to $32 billion in 2025](https://theconversation.com/international-aid-groups-are-dealing-with-the-pain-of-slashed-usaid-funding-by-cutting-staff-local...

editor_notes.content_quality: Important humanitarian tech article covering USAID collapse and AI/corporate response.
editor_notes.source_verification: Semafor, The Conversation, and CNN — all on allowlist or authoritative.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: USAID funding cuts and Lancet projections from major news outlets.
